mnemonics.title: BPTT Steps
mnemonics.content: FFTT: Forward pass, Find loss, Through time backprop, Tune weights (FFTT = Forward Find Through Tune)

mnemonics.title: RNN Hidden State
mnemonics.content: h_t = tanh( x_t·W_xh + h_{t-1}·W_hh + b ) - Think: New = Old + Input, squashed by tanh

acronyms.usage: Training algorithm for RNNs that unfolds the network across time steps
Neural network with cyclic connections for sequential data
Alternative to BPTT with higher complexity O(n³)
RNN variant with gates to combat vanishing gradients
Simplified LSTM variant with update and reset gates

visualTips.visualization: Draw a vertical stack of identical network boxes, one per time step. Connect bottom of each box to top of the next (recurrent connection). Show arrows flowing forward through boxes and backward (dotted) for gradients.
Think of a leaky bucket chain - each time step multiplies gradient by a weight. Farther steps = more multiplications = smaller final gradient (leaky).
Water flowing backward through a series of pipes. Each pipe has a valve (weight) that can reduce or increase flow. Farther from output = more valves to pass through.
Snowball rolling downhill - gains mass (gradient magnitude) exponentially as it rolls (through time steps), eventually becoming uncontrollable.

analogies.analogy: Regular backprop is like climbing a single hill. BPTT is like climbing a hill that's been unrolled into multiple floors - you need to come down each floor to adjust weights.
Like trying to hear a whisper at one end of a long hallway - by the time it reaches the other end, the message has faded to nothing.

analogies.analization: Like a chef following the same recipe at every station in an assembly line - same rules (weights) applied at each step (time step).

analogies.analogy: Like reading a long book but only remembering the last 10 pages when discussing it - you learn from recent context rather than entire book.
